schema_purpose: Authoritative destination schema for AI-assisted data migration — defines all fields, constraints, and transformation rules for the CCH Axcess conversion template

field_definition_spec.field_name: Target column name in the CCH Axcess conversion template / ADU database
field_definition_spec.field_type: Data type: string, int, date, decimal, boolean
field_definition_spec.constraints: Object containing max_length, regex pattern, unique flag, foreign keys, restrict_to picklist references
field_definition_spec.format: Expected format pattern (e.g., MM/DD/YYYY, XX-XXXXXXX, email)
field_definition_spec.required: Whether field is mandatory for CCH import
field_definition_spec.enum: Allowed value list when field is restricted to specific values
field_definition_spec.description: Semantic purpose — used by AI during field matching and discovery
field_definition_spec.transformation_notes: Mechanical rules — used by AI during code generation and data transformation
field_definition_spec.common_source_names: Aliases the AI watches for during source schema discovery (e.g., acct_id, ledger_code)
field_definition_spec.example_values: Concrete sample values for validation and AI pattern matching

global_notes: For any code values, you may provide the old codes and supply a mapping table to the Axcess setup OR you can provide the new codes here
Leading zeros on IDs: ensure file is formatted as Text
Client ID must be consistent across all entities (Client, AR, WIP, Contacts, Billing, Groups)

Client.description: Core client records — one row per client or sub-client

fields.description: Axcess office code identifying the practice office responsible for this client
fields.transformation_notes: Must match an existing Office code in Axcess setup. For acquisitions, map source office/location/branch to Ascend's master office code list.

fields.description: Unique client identifier — primary key linking all client-related data across AR, WIP, Contacts, Billing, and Groups
fields.transformation_notes: Must be alphanumeric string. Preserve leading zeros by formatting as Text. Must be consistent across ALL entity sheets. If source uses numeric IDs, zero-pad or prefix to meet Ascend's ID convention.

fields.description: Subordinate identifier for sub-clients or sub-engagements under a parent Client ID
fields.transformation_notes: Format as Text if leading zeros. Used for multi-entity client families or sub-engagements. Combine with Client ID using dot notation in referencing entities (e.g., 1584.123).

fields.description: Full display name of the client entity
fields.transformation_notes: For individuals, typically 'First Last' or 'Last, First'. For businesses, use legal entity name. Trim whitespace and normalize casing.

fields.description: Alphabetical sort key — typically 'Last, First' for individuals, business name for entities
fields.transformation_notes: For individuals: format as 'LastName, FirstName'. For businesses: use business name. Used for directory listings and report sorting. If source lacks a sort name, derive from client name.

fields.description: Descriptive label for a sub-client record, identifying the purpose of the sub-engagement
fields.transformation_notes: Optional free-text. Typically describes the nature of the sub-engagement (e.g., 'Tax Return', 'Audit', 'Bookkeeping').

fields.description: Legal entity type classification for the client — drives tax form selection and compliance workflows
fields.transformation_notes: Map source entity types to CCH allowed values. Common mappings: 'LLC' -> varies by tax election (Partnership or Corporation), 'Sole Prop' -> 'Individual', '501(c)(3)' -> 'Non-Profit', 'Estate' -> 'Fiduciary'.

fields.description: Current lifecycle status of the client record — controls visibility and billing eligibility
fields.transformation_notes: Map source status values: common mappings include 'Current'/'Open' -> 'Active', 'Closed'/'Terminated' -> 'Inactive', 'Suspended'/'Collections' -> 'On Hold (inactive with balance)'. Clients with outstanding AR balances should use 'On Hold' rather than 'Inactive'.

fields.description: Fiscal year-end month (1-12) — required for Workstream and Workflow scheduling in Axcess
fields.transformation_notes: Integer 1-12. If source stores as date, extract month. If source stores as text (e.g., 'December'), convert to numeric. Calendar year-end clients = 12. Non-standard FYE clients need exact month.

fields.description: Federal Employer Identification Number (EIN) for business entities
fields.transformation_notes: Format as EIN: XX-XXXXXXX. Strip spaces and validate 9-digit format. For individuals, use SSN fields instead. Some source systems store EIN without hyphen — add hyphen at position 2.

fields.description: Primary Social Security Number — typically for the first individual on a joint return
fields.transformation_notes: Format as XXX-XX-XXXX or raw 9 digits depending on source. Handle with PII security protocols. Validate 9-digit format. Strip non-numeric characters before validation.

fields.description: Secondary Social Security Number — typically for the spouse on a joint return
fields.transformation_notes: Same format rules as SSN1. Only populated for joint/married filing clients. Handle with PII security protocols.

constraints.restrict_to: Axcess available Line of Business options

fields.description: Industry classification for the client — used for reporting and engagement categorization
fields.transformation_notes: Must match an Axcess-available Line of Business option. Map source industry codes (NAICS, SIC) or free-text industry to Axcess picklist values.

fields.description: Primary engagement service classification — identifies the main service provided to this client
fields.transformation_notes: Must match an Axcess-available Service Type. Map source service/engagement types to Axcess picklist. Common source values like 'Tax Prep' -> 'Tax', 'Financial Statement' -> 'Audit & Accounting'.

fields.description: Named grouping for client segmentation — used for reporting and batch operations
fields.transformation_notes: Free-text group name. If source uses client categories or tags, map to group names. Groups must be created via Client Group Creation entity before assignment.

fields.description: Client primary address line 1 — street address
fields.transformation_notes: First line of client mailing address. If source has a single address field, parse into Addr1/City/State/Zip components.

fields.description: Client primary address line 2 — suite, floor, or additional address detail
fields.transformation_notes: Optional secondary address line. Common for suite numbers, floor, building name, or c/o.

fields.description: Client primary address line 3 — rarely used overflow address line
fields.transformation_notes: Tertiary address line, seldom populated. Use only when address requires three lines.

fields.description: City component of the client primary address
fields.transformation_notes: Standard city name. Normalize casing (title case). If source has combined city/state, parse and split.

fields.description: State or province component of the client primary address
fields.transformation_notes: Must match Axcess available State/Province options. Convert full state names to abbreviations if Axcess expects codes (e.g., 'North Carolina' -> 'NC').

fields.description: ZIP or postal code component of the client primary address
fields.transformation_notes: Format as string to preserve leading zeros. US: 5-digit or ZIP+4 (XXXXX-XXXX). Validate format matches country.

fields.description: Country component of the client primary address
fields.transformation_notes: Must match Axcess available Country options. Default to 'USA' or 'US' for domestic clients if source is blank. Normalize country names to Axcess codes.

fields.description: Attention line for client correspondence — directs mail to a specific person
fields.transformation_notes: Free-text. Typically a contact name prefixed with 'Attn:' in the source. Strip the 'Attn:' prefix if present in source data.

fields.description: Greeting line for client correspondence (e.g., 'Dear Mr. Smith')
fields.transformation_notes: Free-text salutation. If source lacks this, can be derived from client name and entity type.

fields.description: Billing address line 1 — may differ from client primary address
fields.transformation_notes: If source has no separate billing address, leave blank and Axcess uses client address. Only populate if billing address differs from client address.

fields.description: Billing address line 2
fields.transformation_notes: Secondary billing address line. Same rules as Client Addr2.

fields.description: Billing address line 3
fields.transformation_notes: Tertiary billing address line. Rarely used.

fields.description: City component of the billing address
fields.transformation_notes: Same normalization rules as Client City.

fields.description: State or province component of the billing address
fields.transformation_notes: Must match Axcess available State/Province options.

fields.description: ZIP or postal code of the billing address
fields.transformation_notes: Format as string. Same rules as Client Zip.

fields.description: Country component of the billing address
fields.transformation_notes: Must match Axcess available Country options.

fields.description: Flag indicating whether this client is eligible for billing — controls WIP and invoice generation
fields.transformation_notes: Map to 'Y' or 'N'. Source values like true/false, 1/0, 'Yes'/'No', 'Billable'/'Non-billable' all map here. Default to 'Y' if source has no equivalent field.

fields.description: Finance charge configuration or rate applied to overdue AR balances
fields.transformation_notes: Typically a rate or type identifier. Source may store as percentage or reference to a charge schedule.

fields.description: Date the client became active in the system — required for all client records
fields.transformation_notes: Convert to MM/DD/YYYY format. If source lacks an explicit active date, use the earliest transaction date or engagement start date. Cannot be null.

fields.description: Date the client was deactivated — only for Inactive or On Hold clients
fields.transformation_notes: Convert to MM/DD/YYYY format. Leave blank for Active clients. If source has a closed/terminated date, use that.

fields.description: Primary phone number — type auto-assigned based on client entity type (Business for entities, Home for individuals)
fields.transformation_notes: Normalize to consistent format. Phone type is auto-determined: Business clients -> Business Phone, Individual clients -> Home Phone. Strip extensions into separate handling if needed.

fields.description: Secondary phone number
fields.transformation_notes: Same format rules as Phone 1. Typically a secondary contact number.

fields.description: Tertiary phone number
fields.transformation_notes: Same format rules as Phone 1. Rarely populated.

fields.description: Client fax number
fields.transformation_notes: Standard phone format. Fax numbers are declining in usage — may be blank for many modern clients.

fields.description: Primary client email address — single address only
fields.transformation_notes: Must be a single valid email address. If source has multiple emails, use the primary/business email. Strip whitespace. Axcess does not allow non-email text in this field.

fields.description: Client website URL
fields.transformation_notes: Optional. Include protocol prefix if available (https://). Free-text URL field.

fields.description: Flag indicating whether this is the principal/master record in a client family or group
fields.transformation_notes: Map to 'Y' or 'N'. In multi-entity client families, the parent/master record is 'Y', sub-clients are 'N'. If source has no hierarchy concept, default to 'Y'.

fields.description: Client Responsible Staff role 1 — references a Staff ID configured in Axcess (e.g., Primary Partner, Manager, Tax Reviewer)
fields.transformation_notes: Must match a Staff ID in Axcess setup. Provide CRS mapping in Checklist File. Map source staff assignments (partner, manager, reviewer) to Axcess staff IDs. CRS role definitions (what role CRS1 represents) are configured at the firm level.

fields.description: Client Responsible Staff role 2 — references a Staff ID configured in Axcess (e.g., Primary Partner, Manager, Tax Reviewer)
fields.transformation_notes: Must match a Staff ID in Axcess setup. Provide CRS mapping in Checklist File. Map source staff assignments (partner, manager, reviewer) to Axcess staff IDs. CRS role definitions (what role CRS2 represents) are configured at the firm level.

fields.description: Client Responsible Staff role 3 — references a Staff ID configured in Axcess (e.g., Primary Partner, Manager, Tax Reviewer)
fields.transformation_notes: Must match a Staff ID in Axcess setup. Provide CRS mapping in Checklist File. Map source staff assignments (partner, manager, reviewer) to Axcess staff IDs. CRS role definitions (what role CRS3 represents) are configured at the firm level.

fields.description: Client Responsible Staff role 4 — references a Staff ID configured in Axcess (e.g., Primary Partner, Manager, Tax Reviewer)
fields.transformation_notes: Must match a Staff ID in Axcess setup. Provide CRS mapping in Checklist File. Map source staff assignments (partner, manager, reviewer) to Axcess staff IDs. CRS role definitions (what role CRS4 represents) are configured at the firm level.

fields.description: Client Responsible Staff role 5 — references a Staff ID configured in Axcess (e.g., Primary Partner, Manager, Tax Reviewer)
fields.transformation_notes: Must match a Staff ID in Axcess setup. Provide CRS mapping in Checklist File. Map source staff assignments (partner, manager, reviewer) to Axcess staff IDs. CRS role definitions (what role CRS5 represents) are configured at the firm level.

fields.description: Client Responsible Staff role 6 — references a Staff ID configured in Axcess (e.g., Primary Partner, Manager, Tax Reviewer)
fields.transformation_notes: Must match a Staff ID in Axcess setup. Provide CRS mapping in Checklist File. Map source staff assignments (partner, manager, reviewer) to Axcess staff IDs. CRS role definitions (what role CRS6 represents) are configured at the firm level.

fields.description: Client Responsible Staff role 7 — references a Staff ID configured in Axcess (e.g., Primary Partner, Manager, Tax Reviewer)
fields.transformation_notes: Must match a Staff ID in Axcess setup. Provide CRS mapping in Checklist File. Map source staff assignments (partner, manager, reviewer) to Axcess staff IDs. CRS role definitions (what role CRS7 represents) are configured at the firm level.

fields.description: Client identifier linking this AR transaction to the client record
fields.transformation_notes: Must match a Client ID in the Client entity. Same formatting rules (Text, leading zeros).

fields.description: Sub-client identifier for AR transactions — links to a specific sub-engagement
fields.transformation_notes: Must match Client Sub-ID in Client entity. Required field in template even if blank.

fields.description: Type classification for the AR transaction — determines how the transaction affects client balances
fields.transformation_notes: Map source transaction types to CCH enum. Common mappings: 'INV'/'Bill' -> 'Invoice', 'PMT'/'Receipt' -> 'Payment', 'CM'/'Credit Memo' -> 'Credit', 'DM' -> 'Debit', 'WO' -> 'Writeoff', 'FC'/'Late Fee' -> 'Finance Charge'.

fields.description: Check or payment reference number — numeric identifier for the payment instrument
fields.transformation_notes: Numeric only — strip any non-numeric characters. For electronic payments, use a reference number. Required even for non-payment types (use invoice number or sequence).

fields.description: Invoice number associated with this AR transaction — numeric only
fields.transformation_notes: Numeric only — strip prefixes like 'INV-' or 'I'. Must be a valid integer. Payments should reference the invoice they apply to.

fields.description: Transaction date — the effective date of the AR entry
fields.transformation_notes: Convert to MM/DD/YYYY. This is the primary date for the transaction, not the posting or check date.

fields.description: Check date — the date on the payment instrument. Required for Payment-type transactions
fields.transformation_notes: Convert to MM/DD/YYYY. Required when ARType is 'Payment'. For non-payment types, may be left blank.

fields.description: Posting date — the date the transaction was posted to the ledger
fields.transformation_notes: Convert to MM/DD/YYYY. May differ from TransDate for accrual-based entries. If source has no separate post date, can mirror TransDate.

fields.description: Transaction amount — positive for invoices/debits, negative for payments/credits
fields.transformation_notes: Decimal value. Payments must be negative. If source stores payments as positive, negate them. Ensure sign conventions match: Invoice (+), Payment (-), Credit (-), Debit (+), Writeoff (-), Finance Charge (+).

fields.description: Tax amount included in or added to the AR transaction
fields.transformation_notes: Decimal value. If source does not break out tax separately, set to 0 or null.

fields.description: Finance charge amount assessed on overdue balance
fields.transformation_notes: Decimal value. Typically only populated for Finance Charge type transactions.

fields.description: Progress billing amount — partial billing on an engagement before completion
fields.transformation_notes: Decimal value. Used for progress/retainer billing models. If source doesn't support progress billing, leave null.

fields.description: Amount of progress billing that has been applied against final invoices
fields.transformation_notes: Decimal value. Tracks how much of the progress/retainer has been consumed. If not tracked separately in source, leave null.

fields.description: Client identifier linking this WIP entry to the client record
fields.transformation_notes: Must match a Client ID in the Client entity.

fields.description: Sub-client identifier for WIP transactions
fields.transformation_notes: Must match Client Sub-ID in Client entity if used.

fields.description: Transaction date of the time or expense entry
fields.transformation_notes: Convert to MM/DD/YYYY. The date the work was performed or expense incurred.

fields.description: Identifier of the staff member who performed the work — references Staff setup in Axcess
fields.transformation_notes: Old source codes accepted if a mapping table is provided to the Axcess setup team. Map source employee/staff identifiers to Axcess Staff IDs.

fields.description: Service/activity code classifying the type of work performed — references Service Code setup in Axcess
fields.transformation_notes: Old source codes accepted if a mapping table is provided. Map source activity/task/service codes to Axcess Service Codes.

fields.description: Flag indicating whether this WIP entry is billable to the client
fields.transformation_notes: Map to 'Y' (billable) or 'N' (non-billable). Source values like true/false, 1/0, 'B'/'NB', 'Chargeable'/'Non-chargeable' all map here.

fields.description: Number of hours worked for time entries
fields.transformation_notes: Decimal value (e.g., 1.5 for 1 hour 30 minutes). If source stores in minutes, divide by 60. For expense entries, hours may be 0.

fields.description: Fee amount for the WIP entry — the billable dollar value of the work
fields.transformation_notes: Decimal value. For time entries: typically Hours × Rate. Optional if non-billable. If source stores fees separately from hours×rate, use the fee amount.

fields.description: Expense amount for cost-type WIP entries (e.g., filing fees, postage)
fields.transformation_notes: Decimal value. Used for non-time charges like filing fees, copies, postage. Separate from fee-based time entries.

fields.description: Billing rate per hour for the staff member on this entry
fields.transformation_notes: Decimal value. If source tracks standard vs. actual rate, use the rate that was actually applied. Optional field.

fields.description: Internal cost amount for the WIP entry — used for profitability analysis
fields.transformation_notes: Decimal value. Typically Hours × Staff Cost Rate. If source doesn't track cost rates, may be null.

fields.description: Unit quantity for expense-type entries (e.g., number of copies, miles)
fields.transformation_notes: Decimal value. Expenses only — tracks the quantity of the expense item. For time entries, use Hours instead.

fields.description: Free-text description of the work performed or expense incurred
fields.transformation_notes: Free-text. Truncate if source memo exceeds Axcess field length. Preserve meaningful work descriptions for client-facing invoices.
example_values: Filing fee - NC Secretary of State

fields.description: Amount that was actually billed for this WIP entry — required if converting historical data
fields.transformation_notes: Decimal value. Required if the conversion includes historical billed data. Note: Mango, Onvio, and QuickBooks do not offer history export — this field will be empty for those sources.

fields.description: Progress billing amount applied to this WIP entry
fields.transformation_notes: Decimal value. For progress/retainer billing. If source doesn't track progress billing at the WIP level, leave null.

fields.description: Surcharge amount added to the WIP entry (e.g., technology surcharge)
fields.transformation_notes: Decimal value. Optional. Only populate if source tracks surcharges at the transaction level.

fields.description: Write-up or write-down adjustment amount on the WIP entry
fields.transformation_notes: Decimal value. Positive for write-ups, negative for write-downs. Captures billing adjustments from standard fees.

fields.description: Invoice number this WIP entry was billed on — links WIP to AR
fields.transformation_notes: Integer. Only populated for billed WIP entries. Must match an Invoice Number in the AR entity if cross-referencing.

fields.description: Date the WIP entry was invoiced
fields.transformation_notes: Convert to MM/DD/YYYY. Only populated for billed WIP entries.

Contacts (CCIU).description: Client contact records with multi-client assignment support

fields.description: Client ID that originally owns this contact record — supports Sub-ID notation (e.g., 1584.123)
fields.transformation_notes: Format: Client ID alone or Client ID.Sub-ID (dot-separated). Must reference an existing Client ID in the Client entity.

fields.description: Contact's first/given name
fields.transformation_notes: Trim whitespace. Title case preferred. Part of deduplication key with Middle Name, Last Name, Honorific, Suffix.

fields.description: Contact's last/family name
fields.transformation_notes: Trim whitespace. Part of deduplication key.

fields.format: Comma-separated, double-quoted list of Client IDs
fields.description: Comma-separated list of Client IDs this contact is assigned to — enables multi-client contact sharing
fields.transformation_notes: Format: double-quoted, comma-separated Client IDs. Example: "123456","789012". Deduplication checks against first, middle, last name, honorific, and suffix.

fields.description: Title prefix (e.g., Mr., Mrs., Dr.) — part of deduplication key
fields.transformation_notes: Standard honorific abbreviations. Part of contact dedup key.

fields.description: Name suffix (e.g., Jr., III, Esq.) — part of deduplication key
fields.transformation_notes: Standard suffix abbreviations. Part of contact dedup key.

fields.description: Contact's middle name or initial — part of deduplication key
fields.transformation_notes: Can be full middle name or initial. Part of contact dedup key.

fields.description: Category classification for the contact within the firm's taxonomy
fields.transformation_notes: Free-text. Map source contact categories if they exist.

fields.description: Correspondence greeting for the contact (max 64 characters)
fields.transformation_notes: Max 64 characters. If source lacks salutation, can be derived from name fields.

fields.description: Contact's professional title or position (max 50 characters)
fields.transformation_notes: Max 50 characters. Truncate if source exceeds limit.

fields.description: Comma-separated list of email type classifications matching Axcess picklist
fields.transformation_notes: Must match Axcess available Email Types. Comma-separated, positionally paired with Emails field.

fields.description: Comma-separated list of email addresses — positionally paired with Email Types
fields.transformation_notes: Must be valid email addresses only — Axcess rejects non-email text. Comma-separated, one per Email Type.

fields.description: Flag indicating contact shares the same phone numbers as the client record
fields.transformation_notes: Boolean T/F. If true, phone fields are inherited from client record.

fields.format: Comma-separated list of Axcess phone types
fields.description: Comma-separated list of phone type classifications matching Axcess picklist
fields.transformation_notes: Must match Axcess available Phone Types. Positionally paired with Phones field.

fields.description: Comma-separated phone numbers — positionally paired with Phone Types
fields.transformation_notes: Supported formats: USA 1234567890x123, 234-567-8900, (123) 456-7890x000. Comma-separated.

fields.description: Flag indicating contact shares the same address as the client record
fields.transformation_notes: Boolean T/F. If true, address fields are inherited from client record.

fields.format: Comma-separated list of Axcess address types
fields.description: Comma-separated list of address type classifications matching Axcess picklist
fields.transformation_notes: Must match Axcess available Address Types. Positionally paired with Addresses field.

fields.description: Colon-delimited address blocks — complex multi-field format with positional segments
fields.transformation_notes: Format: AddrL1:AddrL2:AddrL3:AptOrSuite:City:StateOrRegion:PostalCode:CountryCode:PrimaryAddress:PrimaryMailingAddress. If any segment contains a comma, wrap in double quotes. Multiple addresses comma-separated.

fields.format: Comma-separated list of Axcess IM types
fields.description: Comma-separated list of IM type classifications matching Axcess picklist
fields.transformation_notes: Must match Axcess available IM Types. Positionally paired with Instant Messengers and Providers.

fields.description: Comma-separated IM profile names/handles
fields.transformation_notes: Positionally paired with IM Types and Providers. Free-text usernames.

fields.description: Comma-separated IM provider names matching Axcess picklist — required if IM profiles are provided
fields.transformation_notes: Must match Axcess available IM Providers. Required whenever Instant Messengers field is populated.

Client Billing General Info.description: Billing configuration per client — controls invoicing, surcharges, limits

fields.description: Client identifier for billing configuration
fields.transformation_notes: Must match Client entity. Same formatting rules.

fields.description: Sub-client identifier for billing configuration
fields.transformation_notes: Must match Client entity if used.

fields.description: Whether this client/sub-client is eligible for billing
fields.transformation_notes: Boolean: 'True' or 'False'. Maps from source billable flags.

fields.description: Whether AR statements should be printed for this client
fields.transformation_notes: Boolean: 'True' or 'False'.

fields.description: Whether surcharges should be auto-calculated for this client
fields.transformation_notes: Boolean: 'True' or 'False'.

fields.description: Named finance charge schedule applied to this client's overdue balances
fields.transformation_notes: Must match a Finance Charge Type name configured in Axcess.

fields.description: Language code for client-facing documents and correspondence
fields.transformation_notes: Language code per Axcess options. Default to English if source has no equivalent.

fields.description: Name of the invoice template to use for this client
fields.transformation_notes: Must match an Invoice Template name configured in Axcess.

fields.description: Numeric sort code (1-99) controlling the order of billing entries
fields.transformation_notes: Integer 1-99. Controls sort order on invoices and billing reports.

fields.description: Maximum AR balance threshold — triggers alerts when exceeded
fields.transformation_notes: Decimal value. Optional credit limit on outstanding AR.

fields.description: Maximum WIP balance threshold — triggers alerts when exceeded
fields.transformation_notes: Decimal value. Optional cap on unbilled WIP.

fields.description: Maximum write-down amount allowed before requiring approval
fields.transformation_notes: Decimal value. Controls authorization thresholds for fee write-downs.

fields.description: WIP reserve balance — amount set aside against potential unbilled write-offs
fields.transformation_notes: Decimal value. Accounting reserve against WIP.

fields.description: Fixed or recurring billing fee amount for the client
fields.transformation_notes: Decimal value. Used for flat-fee or recurring billing arrangements.

Address Invoice and Statements.description: Invoice and statement delivery routing — controls who receives paper/email and at which address

foreign_keys.field: Invoice Client Contact First Name + Last Name
Statement Client Contact First Name + Last Name

fields.description: Combined client identifier in dot notation — Client ID alone or Client ID.Sub-ID
fields.transformation_notes: Format: without sub = '123456', with sub = '123456.789'. Must reference existing Client entity records.

fields.description: Whether to use a specific contact (vs. the client record) as the invoice recipient
fields.transformation_notes: Boolean: 'True' or 'False'. If True, Invoice Client Contact First/Last Name fields identify the recipient. If False, invoices go to the client record address.

fields.description: First name of the contact designated as invoice recipient — references Contacts (CCIU)
fields.transformation_notes: Must match a contact's First Name in Contacts (CCIU) entity. Only required when 'Use Contact as Invoice Recipient' is True.

fields.description: Last name of the contact designated as invoice recipient — references Contacts (CCIU)
fields.transformation_notes: Must match a contact's Last Name in Contacts (CCIU) entity. Paired with First Name for contact lookup.

fields.description: Address type to use for invoice delivery (e.g., Business, Home)
fields.transformation_notes: Must match an Axcess available Address Type. Determines which address from the contact/client record is used for invoices.

fields.description: Whether to generate a printed paper invoice for this client
fields.transformation_notes: Boolean: 'True' or 'False'. Controls physical mail delivery of invoices.

fields.description: Whether to use a specific contact (vs. the client record) as the AR statement recipient
fields.transformation_notes: Boolean: 'True' or 'False'. If True, Statement Client Contact First/Last Name fields identify the recipient.

fields.description: First name of the contact designated as AR statement recipient — references Contacts (CCIU)
fields.transformation_notes: Must match a contact's First Name in Contacts (CCIU) entity. Only required when 'Use Contact as Statement Recipient' is True.

fields.description: Last name of the contact designated as AR statement recipient — references Contacts (CCIU)
fields.transformation_notes: Must match a contact's Last Name in Contacts (CCIU) entity.

fields.description: Address type to use for statement delivery (e.g., Business, Home)
fields.transformation_notes: Must match an Axcess available Address Type.

fields.description: Whether to generate a printed paper AR statement for this client
fields.transformation_notes: Boolean: 'True' or 'False'. Controls physical mail delivery of statements.

fields.description: Whether to save a copy of invoices/statements to the document management system
fields.transformation_notes: Boolean: 'True' or 'False'. When True, Axcess stores a copy in the client's document folder.

Client Email Invoices.description: Email-based invoice and statement delivery per client email type

fields.description: Combined client identifier in dot notation — Client ID alone or Client ID.Sub-ID
fields.transformation_notes: Format: without sub = '123456', with sub = '123456.789'. Must reference existing Client entity records.

fields.description: Client sort name for reference — informational field to help identify the client row
fields.transformation_notes: Informational/display field. Should match the Sort Name from the Client entity. Not used for matching.

fields.description: Email type classification (e.g., Home, Business) — determines which client email address receives invoices/statements
fields.transformation_notes: Must match an Email Type configured on the client record. Paired with Receive Invoice and Receive Statement flags to control per-type delivery.

fields.description: Whether this email type should receive invoice emails for the client
fields.transformation_notes: Boolean: 'True' or 'False'. Controls email delivery of invoices per email type.

fields.description: Whether this email type should receive AR statement emails for the client
fields.transformation_notes: Boolean: 'True' or 'False'. Controls email delivery of statements per email type.

Contact Email Invoices.description: Email-based invoice and statement delivery per individual contact

fields.description: Client ID this contact email routing applies to — the client whose invoices/statements this contact should receive
fields.transformation_notes: Format: Client ID alone or Client ID.Sub-ID. Must reference an existing Client entity record.

fields.description: Contact honorific/title — required if the contact record in CCIU uses an honorific (for dedup matching)
fields.transformation_notes: Must match the Honorific on the contact record in Contacts (CCIU) if one exists. Part of contact identification.

fields.description: First name of the contact — must match a contact in Contacts (CCIU) entity
fields.transformation_notes: Must exactly match the First Name in Contacts (CCIU) for contact lookup.

fields.description: Contact middle name — required if the contact record in CCIU uses a middle name (for dedup matching)
fields.transformation_notes: Must match the Middle Name on the contact record if one exists.

fields.description: Last name of the contact — must match a contact in Contacts (CCIU) entity
fields.transformation_notes: Must exactly match the Last Name in Contacts (CCIU) for contact lookup.

fields.description: Contact suffix — required if the contact record in CCIU uses a suffix (for dedup matching)
fields.transformation_notes: Must match the Suffix on the contact record if one exists.

fields.description: The Client ID where this contact was originally created — used with name fields for unique contact identification
fields.transformation_notes: Format: Client ID alone or Client ID.Sub-ID. References the contact's home client record in Contacts (CCIU).

fields.description: Email type classification for this contact's email (e.g., Home, Business)
fields.transformation_notes: Must match an Email Type configured on the contact record in Contacts (CCIU).

fields.description: The specific email address to use for invoice/statement delivery to this contact
fields.transformation_notes: Must be a valid email address. Must match an email on the contact record in Contacts (CCIU).

fields.description: Whether this contact email should receive invoice emails
fields.transformation_notes: Boolean: 'True' or 'False'.

fields.description: Whether this contact email should receive AR statement emails
fields.transformation_notes: Boolean: 'True' or 'False'.

Client Custom Field Values.description: Firm-defined custom fields per client — flexible key-value store

value_types.format: Valid value from custom value list
Any text value, max length of field in Lists

fields.description: Name of the custom field as defined in Axcess Lists setup — the key in the key-value pair
fields.transformation_notes: Must exactly match a custom field name configured in Axcess. Custom fields are firm-defined and vary by Axcess instance. Verify available custom fields during discovery.

fields.format: Depends on value type — see value_types
fields.description: Value for the custom field — format depends on the field's value type (Checkbox, Date, Currency, Text, etc.)
fields.transformation_notes: Format varies by value type: Checkbox='T'/'F', Date='m/d/yyyy', Currency=decimal, Number=integer, Text=free text, Staff List=Staff ID, Client List=Client Sort Name. See entity value_types spec for complete format rules.

fields.description: Client identifier this custom field value belongs to
fields.transformation_notes: Must match Client entity. Standard formatting rules.

fields.description: Sub-client identifier this custom field value belongs to
fields.transformation_notes: Must match Client entity if used.

Billing Group Renames.description: Rename existing billing groups by GUID reference

fields.description: New display name for an existing billing group (max 45 characters)
fields.transformation_notes: Max 45 characters. Used to rename billing groups during migration — the group is identified by GUID, not old name.

fields.description: Updated description for the billing group being renamed
fields.transformation_notes: Free-text description. Optional.
example_values: Clients billed on monthly retainer basis

fields.description: Globally unique identifier of the existing billing group to rename — obtained from Axcess
fields.transformation_notes: Must be a valid GUID from the existing Axcess billing group setup. Cannot be fabricated — must be extracted from Axcess.

fields.description: Action to perform: create a new billing group or remove an existing one
fields.transformation_notes: Enum: 'Add' to create, 'Remove' to delete. Removal requires no active client assignments.

fields.description: Name of the billing group to create or remove (max 45 characters)
fields.transformation_notes: Max 45 characters. Must be unique across billing groups. Used as the key for Billing Group Assignments.

fields.description: Description of the billing group's purpose
fields.transformation_notes: Free-text. Optional.
example_values: Clients prioritized during tax season billing

Billing Group Assignments.description: Assign clients to billing groups with principal designation and billing mode

fields.description: Action to perform: assign a client to or remove a client from a billing group
fields.transformation_notes: Enum: 'Add' or 'Remove'.

fields.description: Name of the billing group to assign the client to — must exist in Billing Group Creation
fields.transformation_notes: Max 45 characters. Must reference an existing billing group from Billing Group Creation entity.

fields.description: Whether this client is the principal/lead client in the billing group — controls group billing behavior
fields.transformation_notes: Boolean. Each billing group should have exactly one principal client. The principal receives the consolidated group invoice.

fields.description: Client identifier being assigned to the billing group
fields.transformation_notes: Must match Client entity.

fields.description: Sub-client identifier being assigned to the billing group
fields.transformation_notes: Must match Client entity if used.

fields.description: Whether this client should be billed individually or as part of the group on consolidated invoices
fields.transformation_notes: Enum: 'Individual' for separate invoices, 'Group' for consolidated billing through the principal client.

fields.description: Action to perform: create a new client group or remove an existing one
fields.transformation_notes: Enum: 'Add' to create, 'Remove' to delete.

fields.description: Name of the client group to create or remove (max 45 characters)
fields.transformation_notes: Max 45 characters. Must be unique across client groups. Used as the key for Client Group Assignments.

fields.description: Description of the client group's purpose
fields.transformation_notes: Free-text. Optional.
example_values: Clients with >$1M in assets under advisement

Client Group Assignments.description: Assign clients to client groups with financial reporting flag

fields.description: Action to perform: assign a client to or remove a client from a client group
fields.transformation_notes: Enum: 'Add' or 'Remove'.

fields.description: Name of the client group to assign the client to — must exist in Client Group Creation
fields.transformation_notes: Max 45 characters. Must reference an existing client group from Client Group Creation entity.

fields.description: Whether this group is used for financial reporting consolidation purposes
fields.transformation_notes: Boolean: 'True' or 'False'. Financial reporting groups enable consolidated financial reporting across grouped clients.

fields.description: Client identifier being assigned to the client group
fields.transformation_notes: Must match Client entity.

fields.description: Sub-client identifier being assigned to the client group
fields.transformation_notes: Must match Client entity if used.

Client Primary Contact Info.description: Set primary contact type preferences per client — which email, address, phone type is primary
special_notes: Blank cells are ignored — they will not unset the primary on the client
To unset the primary, place <Remove> in the cell

fields.description: Client identifier for primary contact info configuration
fields.transformation_notes: Must match Client entity. Format as Text for leading zeros.

fields.description: Sub-client identifier for primary contact info configuration
fields.transformation_notes: Must match Client entity if used. Format as Text for leading zeros.

fields.description: Client sort name for reference — informational field to identify the client row
fields.transformation_notes: Should match the Sort Name from the Client entity. Informational only.

fields.description: Entity type code for the client — determines available contact type defaults and admin/sponsor options
fields.transformation_notes: Must use the exact code-label format: 'IN - Individual', 'CR - Corporation', etc. Map from source entity types. This field controls downstream behavior like Admin/Sponsor eligibility.

fields.description: Email type to set as the client's primary email (e.g., Business, Home)
fields.transformation_notes: Must match an Email Type already configured on the client. Blank = no change; '<Remove>' = unset primary.

fields.description: Address type to set as the client's primary address (e.g., Business)
fields.transformation_notes: Must match an Address Type already configured on the client. Blank = no change; '<Remove>' = unset primary.

fields.description: Address type to set as the client's mailing address — may differ from primary
fields.transformation_notes: Must match an Address Type already configured on the client. Blank = no change; '<Remove>' = unset mailing.

fields.description: Phone type to set as the client's primary phone (e.g., Mobile, Business)
fields.transformation_notes: Must match a Phone Type already configured on the client. Blank = no change; '<Remove>' = unset primary.

fields.description: IM type and provider pairs to configure for this client — comma-separated Type:Provider format
fields.transformation_notes: Format: 'Type:Provider' pairs, comma-separated. Example: 'Home:AIM' or 'Business:PalTalk,Home:AIM'.

fields.description: Admin or Sponsor designation — only applicable for Employee Plan or Custom entity type clients
fields.transformation_notes: Enum: 'ADMIN' or 'SPNSR'. Only set for clients with Client Type Code of 'EP - Employee Plan'. Leave blank for all other entity types.

relationships.summary: Client is the central entity. All other entities reference Client via Client ID (and optionally Client Sub-ID). Groups and Billing Groups are created independently then assigned to clients. Contacts can be assigned to multiple clients and are referenced by invoice/statement routing entities.

external_references.references: Staff table (not in conversion template — setup separately in Axcess)
Service Code table (not in conversion template — setup separately in Axcess)
Staff ID setup in Axcess (not in conversion template)
Department setup in Axcess (not in conversion template)
Office setup in Axcess (not in conversion template)

import_order.description: Suggested import sequence based on dependency chain

sequence.reason: All other entities reference Client ID
Sets primary types after contacts exist
